question.text: In an options transaction, the amount paid by the buyer to the seller for the right to buy or sell is called:

explanation.text: **The Logic:** The amount paid by the option buyer to the seller (writer) for the right to buy (call) or sell (put) is called the Option Premium. It is non-refundable. **The Trap:** Margin is the deposit for futures trading. Strike/exercise price is the agreed transaction price. Settlement price is the closing price used for daily settlement. **Cross-Exam Context:** Series VIII (Equity Derivatives) and Series I (Currency Derivatives) cover options premium in detail. **Distributor's Pro-Tip:** The premium is the maximum loss for an option buyer — this limited downside is what makes options attractive for hedging compared to futures.
